title: API Style Guide Rule
description: A single normative rule extracted from a published API style guide (e.g. a Zalando numbered rule, a Google AIP, a Microsoft Guidelines section). Designed to make rules from heterogeneous guides comparable and lintable.

id.description: Stable identifier for the rule, unique within the guide. Examples: 'zalando-100', 'aip-131', 'ms-azure-versioning', 'rfc-9457'.

guide.description: Identifier of the parent style guide. Should match an aid value in apis.yml.

level.description: Normative strength of the rule. Aligned with RFC 2119 / RFC 8174 keywords.

title.description: Short imperative statement of the rule, in Title Case where idiomatic. e.g. 'Follow API First Principle', 'Use Title Case for Operation Summaries'.

summary.description: One-paragraph plain-language summary of what the rule says and why it exists.

rationale.description: The motivating principle: why the guide adopts this rule. Often anchors to consumer experience, evolvability, or interoperability.

appliesTo.description: Which API surfaces the rule applies to.

references.description: Authoritative references the rule cites (RFCs, OpenAPI sections, other style guide rules).

examples.description: Concrete good / bad examples illustrating the rule.

language.description: Language of the snippet, e.g. http, json, yaml, openapi.

spectralRule.description: Optional mapping to a Spectral lint rule that operationalizes this style guide rule against an OpenAPI document.

given.description: JSONPath expression for the Spectral 'given' field.

supersedes.description: IDs of older rules this rule replaces.

relatedRules.description: Cross-references to rules in other guides that cover the same concern (e.g. errors).

sourceUrl.description: Direct URL to the rule's section in its source style guide.
